texto: When he appeared before the lord, his lordship was smitten immediately with the boy's unadorned beauty, like a first glimpse of the moon rising above a distant mountain. The boy's hair gleamed like the feathers of a raven perched silently on a tree, and his eyes were lovely as lotus flowers. One by one his other qualities became apparent, from his nightingale voice to his gentle disposition, as obedient and true as a plum blossom.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Saikaku Ihara: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “When he appeared before the lord, his lordship was smitten immediately with the boy's unadorned b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, beauty, boy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: When he appeared before the lord, his lordship was smitten immediately with the boy's unadorned beauty, like a first glimpse of the moon rising above a distant mountain. The boy's hair gleamed like the feathers of a raven perched silently on a tree, and his eyes were lovely as lotus flowers. One by one his other qualities became apparent, from his nightingale voice to his gentle disposition, as obedient and true as a plum blossom.
